channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ombudsman Remulla criticizes Senator Dela Rosa for evading ICC arrest warrant
item.description: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla criticized Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa for allegedly evading an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court (ICC) related to alleged crimes against hu...

item.title: NEET UG 2026 Re-Examination Scheduled on June 21 Following Paper Leak Investigation
item.description: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has officially announced that the NEET UG 2026 examination will be re-conducted on Sunday, 21st June 2026. This decision follows the cancellation of the exam held on ...

item.title: Labour’s Purpose in 2026: Challenges for Leadership and Representation
item.description: The Guardian columnist Gaby Hinsliff explores the ongoing uncertainty within the Labour Party as potential leadership candidates prepare to challenge Keir Starmer. Wes Streeting’s dramatic resignation...

item.title: UK Nuclear Regulatory Taskforce Faces Criticism Over Expertise and Recommendations
item.description: The UK government's 2025 Nuclear Regulatory Taskforce, created to streamline regulations for expanding nuclear power, has faced significant criticism. The taskforce, led by economist John Fingleton, i...

item.title: Shivo Tanzania achieves strong early-stage growth in Paje property projects
item.description: Shivo Tanzania, a real estate developer based in Zanzibar, has shown impressive growth in its early years of operation, reflecting strong investor confidence in the island’s property market. Over two ...

item.title: Gold Coast Suns team manager cleared by AFL after bookmaker involvement; Victoria regulator reopens probe
item.description: The AFL has officially cleared Mark Opie, the Gold Coast Suns team manager, following an investigation by the league's Integrity Unit. The probe was launched after it emerged that Opie co-founded a re...

item.title: Bernardo Silva Reflects on Leadership, Culture and Set-Piece Changes at Manchester City Before Departure
item.description: Bernardo Silva, who is set to leave Manchester City this summer after nine years, spoke candidly about his career, leadership, and the club’s evolving culture. Having made 457 appearances and won 15 m...

item.title: Jinggoy Estrada denies allegations regarding missing CCTV footage
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada has publicly denied recent claims circulating about a supposed 'itago CCTV' video. According to the reports, the video allegedly showed certain controversial activities, but Es...

item.title: Economic Consequences of the Iran Conflict on Gulf States
item.description: The ongoing war on Iran by the US and Israel has imposed significant economic strain on the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries, including Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia....

item.title: Ramaphosa faces legal and political challenges over Phala Phala and impeachment
item.description: Ahead of South Africa’s local government elections on 4 November 2026, President Cyril Ramaphosa is navigating a complex legal and political landscape following the Constitutional Court’s ruling that ...

item.title: Energy Australia Proposes Expanding Marulan Gas Plant to 1.4 GW Amid Rising Power Demand
item.description: Energy Australia has submitted an application to nearly double the planned capacity of its Marulan gas-fired power station in New South Wales, increasing it from the originally approved 800 megawatts ...

item.title: Joy Weston: Iconic Model and Fashion Influence of the 1950s and 1960s
item.description: Joy Weston was a defining figure in high-fashion modeling during the 1950s and 1960s, capturing the transition from the formal elegance of the post-war era to the playful modernity of the Swinging Six...

item.title: Tanzania aims to boost startups with $50 million Venture Capital Fund
item.description: Tanzania’s startup sector is still struggling compared to other African countries like Kenya, Nigeria, South Africa, and Egypt, despite showing some promise in 2024 when local startups raised $43 mill...

item.title: Vancouver, B.C. Government and First Nations Partner to Retain Whitecaps in the City Long-Term
item.description: Representatives from the Vancouver Whitecaps, Major League Soccer, the City of Vancouver, the B.C. provincial government, the federal government, PavCo, and three local First Nations — Musqueam, Squam...

item.title: Benin City Residents Turn to Traditional Medicine Amid High Healthcare Costs
item.description: People for Benin City, Edo State don dey struggle to manage healthcare as the price of medicine don waka pass normal. Many residents dey complain say because drug prices, hospital fees, and lab charge...

item.title: British Gas Faces £163.2 Million Compensation for Prepayment Meter Customers
item.description: British Gas, one of the UK's largest energy suppliers, has agreed to pay £163.2 million as compensation to customers who were affected by unfair practices related to prepayment meters. This settlement...

item.title: Trump wraps up China visit, discusses trade, AI and regional tensions with Xi
item.description: United States President Donald Trump has wrapped up his official visit to China, saying he secured important trade deals with Chinese President Xi Jinping. During his visit to Beijing, Trump aimed to ...

item.title: Western Cape Minibus Taxi Fares Set to Increase Amid Rising Costs
item.description: Minibus taxi fares in the Western Cape are set to rise, causing concern among commuters already struggling with financial pressures. Local trips will see fare increases ranging from R2 to R5, while lo...

item.title: Trump and Xi Discuss Taiwan, Trade, and Security Concerns in Strategic Summit
item.description: The recent summit between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping addressed several critical global issues, including trade, technology, the conflict in Iran, and maintaining the op...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif Highlights Strong Pak-China Ties in Meeting with Chinese Tech Delegation
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif warmly welcomed a high-level Chinese business delegation in Islamabad on Friday and praised the historic and all-weather strategic partnership between Pakistan and China....
